source: DOE Guidelines, BERDE, ASHRAE 90.1 CZ1A, PGBC

rules.description: Maximum Overall Thermal Transfer Value for air-conditioned buildings
rules.notes: OTTV measures total heat gain through walls. Philippine GBC adopts Singapore SS 530 methodology. OTTV = sum of conduction + solar heat gain through opaque and glazed portions.
Roof receives highest solar radiation. RTTV accounts for roof insulation, absorptance, and any skylight area.

rules.description: Maximum window-to-wall ratio for energy efficiency
rules.notes: Higher WWR increases cooling load. If WWR exceeds 40%, compensate with high-performance glazing (U-value ≤ 2.2 W/m²K, SHGC ≤ 0.25) or external shading.

rules.description: Maximum Solar Heat Gain Coefficient for glazing in tropical climate
rules.notes: SHGC ≤ 0.25 for east/west facades, ≤ 0.40 for north/south with shading. Low-E glass with tinted or reflective coating.
Double-glazed IGU recommended for AC buildings. Single-glazed acceptable only if SHGC ≤ 0.25 and external shading provided.
ASHRAE 90.1 minimum for CZ1A. BERDE requires 10% above minimum for 1 credit, 20% for 2 credits.

rules.description: Minimum EER for unitary/split AC systems (≤19kW)
rules.notes: DOE EE labeling: 5-star inverter units typically achieve EER 12-14. Non-inverter minimum is EER 9.7 per DOE.
DOE recommended setpoint 25°C (not 22-23°C common practice). Each degree below 25°C increases energy consumption by 3-5%. Ceiling fans at 25°C feel like 22°C.

rules.description: Minimum outdoor air ventilation rate per person
rules.notes: ASHRAE 62.1 for offices. Higher in tropics due to humidity — dehumidification of outdoor air is a major energy load. Energy recovery ventilators (ERV) save 30-50%.

rules.description: Maximum Lighting Power Density for offices
rules.notes: ASHRAE 90.1 prescriptive. LED achieves 5-7 W/m² for 500 lux — well below limit. BERDE gives credit for 30% below standard.

rules.description: Maximum Lighting Power Density for residential common areas
rules.notes: Lobbies, corridors, parking. LED fixtures meet this easily.

rules.description: Minimum daylit area as percentage of regularly occupied floor area
rules.notes: BERDE requires 50% of occupied spaces achieve 300-3000 lux daylight. Reduces lighting energy by 40-60%. Pair with automated dimming controls.

rules.description: Occupancy sensors required for intermittent-use spaces
rules.notes: Required for: restrooms, storage, conference rooms, stairwells, corridors. Reduces lighting energy by 15-30% in these areas.

rules.description: Solar PV potential — minimum annual generation per kWp in Philippines
rules.notes: Philippines average: 1400-1600 kWh/kWp/year (among highest globally). Visayas/Mindanao slightly higher than Luzon. 5-6 peak sun hours/day.

rules.description: Solar PV recommended capacity for residential buildings
rules.notes: 2kWp covers ~50% of typical household consumption. Net metering available under RA 9513 (Renewable Energy Act). ROI: 5-7 years in Philippines.

rules.description: Solar water heating recommended for buildings with hot water demand
rules.notes: Evacuated tube collectors preferred in tropics (effective even in cloudy/rainy conditions). 200L system covers 4-person household. BERDE awards 1-2 credits.

rules.description: Minimum on-site renewable energy as percentage of total building energy
rules.notes: BERDE prerequisite for Green Mark certification. 5% is entry level — 10% earns additional credits. Include solar PV, SWH, wind, or biomass.
